finding: B. INTRINSIC AORTIC VALVE DISEASE impairing leaflet function (less common
chapter: Cause: mural thrombus dispersed by delivery system

differentials.name: Myxomatous valve associated with cystic medial necrosis
differentials.raw: Myxomatous valve associated with cystic medial necrosis

differentials.name: Atherosclerotic degeneration ◊  Severely calcified aortic valves are often regurgitant
differentials.raw: Atherosclerotic degeneration ◊  Severely calcified aortic valves are often regurgitant

differentials.name: Systemic inflammation: lupus erythematosus, Crohn disease, Takayasu arteritis, giant cell arteritis
differentials.raw: Systemic inflammation: lupus erythematosus, Crohn disease, Takayasu arteritis, giant cell arteritis

differentials.name: Aortic valve prolapse Pathogenesis:   LV volume overload → progressive enlargement of diastolic + systolic LV dimensions → increase in myocardial fiber length + increase in stroke volume;
differentials.raw: Aortic valve prolapse Pathogenesis:   LV volume overload → progressive enlargement of diastolic + systolic LV dimensions → increase in myocardial fiber length + increase in stroke volume;

differentials.name: systolic ejection murmur + high-pitched diastolic murmur
differentials.raw: systolic ejection murmur + high-pitched diastolic murmur

differentials.name: Austin Flint murmur = soft mid-diastolic or presystolic bruit
differentials.raw: Austin Flint murmur = soft mid-diastolic or presystolic bruit

context: N.B.:   Most common cause in developed nations N.B.:   Most common cause in developing world decompensation occurs if critical limit of fiber length is reached √  LV enlargement (cardiothoracic ratio > 0.55) + initially normal pulmonary vascularity (DDx: congestive cardiomyopathy, pericardial effusion): √  LV wall may appear thin even with ventricular hypertrophy √  normal aorta (in intrinsic valve disease) √  dilatation of aorta (in systemic disease): √  ± calcification of ascending aorta (in aortic wall disease) √  ±   enlarged aortic arch + tortuous descending aorta √  increased pulsations
